methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $43M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
FleetZero, a Series A transportation company founded by Steven Henderson and Mike Carter, has raised $43M to date. The relevant comp universe consists of 8 private frontier-tech players in transportation, including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Pipedream, and Parallel Systems; no public comparables exist in the narrow peer set. No traditional public market anchors are available for this sector.

Valuation analysis relied on a single applicable method due to data constraints. Stage-Progression modeling applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $43M total capital raised as a proxy baseline, implying a post-money valuation of $86M. Peer-Median methodology was not applicable; while three same-sector transportation companies exist, none met the narrow filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparability required to form a reliable median. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable bias.

The triangulated range is $73M (low) to $99M (high), with a median of $86M. This single-method range carries material uncertainty. The analysis is constrained by the absence of closely comparable Series A transportation transactions and the lack of public market validation. Key uncertainties include unit economics relative to the peer set, capital intensity trajectory, and whether the comp universe adequately reflects FleetZero's specific risk profile and market position. A more robust valuation would require either additional transaction data or fundamental metrics-based modeling.
